rationale: The postcondition Antecedent ==> det_equal(r1, r2) is mathematically unprovable. The antecedent only constrains r1.1 via '!r1.0 ==> r1.1 == R' and r2.1 via '!r2.0 ==> r2.1 == R'. When the underlying forall expression Q is true (so r1.0 == r2.0 == true), r1.1 and r2.1 are completely unconstrained, making r1=(true,true), r2=(true,false) a valid counterexample that satisfies the antecedent yet falsifies det_equal. Without using assume/admit/external_body (all forbidden) there is no way to bridge this gap. Tried (1) explicit conjunct re-assertions, (2) direct assertion of the full implication; both produce 'assertion failed' / 'postcondition not satisfied' from the SMT solver, confirming the counterexample. The previous attempt_01 reached the same conclusion.
